security-review._rationale: KNOWN FLAKY: base model already knows security concepts (slopsquatting, hardcoded keys). These markers test skill INVOCATION not knowledge. If skill fires via Skill tool, it announces itself. If loaded via hook only, may not announce. Accept flakiness.
security-review._skip_note: If this test is consistently unreliable, add pytest.mark.skip with reason 'base model security knowledge makes RED-GREEN indistinguishable'

verification-before-completion._rationale: Iron Law and fresh terminal are skill-specific. RED will say 'run tests' without this framework.

test-driven-development._rationale: TDD discipline language. RED may write tests but won't insist on watching failure first.

executing-plans._rationale: UPP-specific execution terminology. RED will describe plan execution without these terms.

finishing-a-development-branch._rationale: Structured options and pre-completion verification are skill outputs.

requesting-code-review._rationale: Evidence bundle and risk tier are skill-specific terminology.
